title: version command with format flag returns unknown shorthand -i flag error
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When you run `podman version --format {{.Server.APIVersion}}` in podman 4.2.0 and 4.1.1, it returns the following error:

```
Error: unknown shorthand flag: 'i' in -inputFormat
See 'podman.exe version --help'
```

This command seems to return the proper value in podman 3.4.4.  While this may seem minor, this incompatibility is how some software projects (like vscode's remote container extension) rely on checking the docker version (to know if docker is installed) and thus this failure causes those systems to think podman isn't installed or working.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.2.0

Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Fri Jul 22 15:05:59 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Doesn't reproduce on Linux. @n1hility Any chance this is something Windows-specific?

Oh I guess this is an issue with powershell messing with the input, presumably cause it uses `{` and `}` for it's own stuff.  So I guess this is a non-issue, or at least a non-podman issue, sorry.  I can get this to work with basic command line in windows.
